summary:
Bilateral cornual block severely impairs fertility because it blocks sperm-egg interaction within the reproductive tract. IVF is optimal as it circumvents the obstructed fallopian tubes through artificial insemination techniques that directly combine eggs and sperm externally. 

The summary above was generated based solely upon Agent2’s provided text; no external information has been added.

Okay, let me go step-by-step according to your instructions:

First, I need only summarize what Agent2 said — not add anything new beyond their original content.

Second, keep strictly two sentences focusing exclusively on clinically significant facts related to managing infertility caused by bilateral cornual block.

Third, ensure clarity about why certain interventions fail and how IVF becomes preferable.

Now reviewing Agent2’s actual reasoning again from Round 1:

They state that bilaterally blocked cornua prevent normal conception via failed sperm/ovum meeting → so procedures targeting tube opening won’t work effectively  
→ Diagnosis may occur via surgery/histero/laparoscopic methods but they don't fix deep-seated issues  
→ Therefore, IVF offers most viable path since it skips entire uterine-tube pathway

So distilled into just two clear, concise statements focused purely on core medical logic without fluff or extra detail:

Final Summary:
Bilateral cornual block impedes natural conception by preventing sperm access to ova due to complete tubal occlusion. In Vitro Fertilization (IVF) provides the highest success rate by allowing controlled union of gametes independent of damaged fallopian tubes.
